strengths: Accurate conceptual definitions of virtue ethics demonstrate a solid theoretical foundation.
Clear identification of the trolley problem as an object helps maintain a coherent structure.
Repeated references to ethical theories show an attempt to engage with the IA prompt.
weaknesses: All three objects are represented by generic images with no specific provenance or realâworld grounding.
Links between each object and the IA prompt remain largely descriptive and repetitive, lacking depth or evidence.
Justifications are thin, unfocused, and do not move beyond restating definitions to show how knowing each object imposes an ethical obligation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition shows minimal grounding of TOK in concrete contexts, with generic images and largely definitional links to the IA prompt, placing it initially in the lower band (1â2). However, the accurate use of ethical theory terminology and the coherent structure across objects demonstrate slightly stronger engagement than a bare Band 1 submission. On balance, this performance merits a score at the lower edge of Band 3â4 (3 marks) to reflect some conceptual strength, while still recognising the many gaps in realâworld linkage, depth of analysis, and evidence support.

annotations.comment: Explicitly discuss how knowledge of the trolley problem itself places ethical obligations on those who learn it (e.g., in education or policy), to link more clearly to the prompt.
The text conflates culture and values without consistently applying TOK terminology (e.g., linking values to shared knower assumptions). A more precise use of concepts would improve clarity.
The structure of each objectâs commentary is very similar, resulting in repetitive framing. Varying the analytical approach (e.g., comparing theories across objects) would add depth.
The student restates the prompt but does not consistently reference it in subsequent analysis. Clearer alignment of each objectâs discussion with the IA prompt would strengthen focus.

The ChatGPT logo is a generic internet image with no specific provenance or location. Providing a real instance (e.g., a photo of a deployed kiosk or a screenshot from your own use) would ground the object in a real-world context.

The explanation of virtue ethics describes definitions but stops short of analyzing how ChatGPT uniquely reveals ethical obligations for those who know AI outputs. More object-specific analysis is needed.
The student offers a clear definition of virtue ethics, demonstrating accurate conceptual understanding. This establishes a solid theoretical foundation.
To deepen the analysis, the student should connect virtue ethics directly to a real ChatGPT case (e.g., misinformation incident), showing how knowledge produced imposes ethical obligations.
The continued ChatGPT discussion reiterates developer obligations with little new insight. This repetition dilutes focus and fails to tie back uniquely to the chosen object.
The âGolden Ruleâ is introduced without stating which real-world instance of this quote inspired the image. Identifying a specific source (e.g., a historical plaque) would clarify context.
The stockâstyle Golden Rule image remains too generic. Connecting it to a tangible artifact (e.g., a community mural) would enhance the objectâs realâworld grounding.
The link to utilitarianism is largely definitional. A deeper connection could examine how a particular application of the Golden Rule produced measurable outcomes in a real context.
To strengthen justification, include a case study of the Golden Rule in practice (e.g., a charity adopting this principle) to show how knowledge of this rule imposes specific obligations.
Although the Golden Rule is explained, the discussion does not address how knowing this principle imposes an obligation to act in real interactions. Explicitly tie back to the prompt.
The trolley problem is clearly identified as Object 3, which helps the reader track the structure across objects.
The trolley problem illustration is schematic and generic. Referencing a documented psychological study or a public art installation would provide a concrete real-world anchor.
Consider presenting alternative perspectives on the trolley problem (e.g., deontological view) to diversify theoretical approaches and avoid repetitive utilitarian focus.
The trolley problem discussion repeats utilitarian definitions without probing how knowing about this thought experiment creates a specific ethical obligation for decision-makers.
One reference entry uses a PMID/PMCID; another uses a URL. Select one referencing style and apply it consistently for all sources.
In-text citations lack consistent style (e.g., missing page numbers, inconsistent bracket use). Adopting a clear citation format will enhance academic rigor.
The listed word count is within the limit, but depth of analysis varies greatly between objects. Redistributing word usage could ensure more balanced treatment.
The theme âKnowledge and the knowerâ is stated but not explicitly woven into each analysis. Refer back to this theme when discussing each object.
How does each chosen object specifically illustrate that all knowledge imposes ethical obligations on those who know it? Making this explicit will strengthen coherence.
The first reference lacks italics for the article title and does not follow a consistent style. Applying a uniform format (e.g., APA) will improve professionalism.
The Toptal blog citation mixes author name with title without standard punctuation. Consistent entry structure (author, date, title, source) is needed.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
